title: Contract Project Manager – Nuclear Decommissioning (Mechanical)
description_raw:
NES Fircroft are supporting a major nuclear decommissioning contractor at Sellafield in the recruitment of a senior Contract Project Manager to lead a complex mechanical decommissioning scope within a Category A nuclear facility.

The scope covers the removal, segregation, and interim storage of large mechanical plant items (pumps, vessels, pipework assemblies, heat exchangers) in radiologically controlled environments. Work is conducted under NDA (Nuclear Decommissioning Authority) governance.

Scope of responsibilities:
- Manage the mechanical decommissioning work package from initiation through completion and close-out
- Prepare and maintain the Master Project Schedule (Primavera P6) and weekly lookout schedule
- Develop Work Package Instructions (WPIs) in accordance with the site’s Intelligent Customer Capability (ICC) framework
- Manage dose budget and ALARP demonstration for all radiological work activities
- Interface with the Sellafield Ltd. Plant Owner and Independent Safety Assessor (ISA) on technical approvals
- Report progress against NDA Lifetime Plan milestones

Profile requirements:
- Degree-level education in mechanical engineering or equivalent
- NVQ Level 7 Project Management or APM PMQ
- Minimum 8 years’ project management in nuclear or high-hazard environment
- SC or DV clearance — must hold or be clearable
- Previous Sellafield or NDA programme experience highly advantageous
- Knowledge of LC (Licence Condition) 28 and LC 36 procedures preferred

Rate: £725 per day, outside IR35
Duration: 18 months (with potential to extend through decommissioning completion milestone)
Start: 1 September 2026
Location: Sellafield site (Cumbria) — fully on-site, no remote option
